author: @lordcheesus1013
text:
Wow. If that's how modern kids think and act, I'm so sorry for future humanity. There aren't words. 

The clear lack of discipline in their households is outrageous. Why do I get the feeling that those two kids didn't get told "No" that much if at all in their lives? 

Hopefully justice prevails and they learn the hard way that actions do in fact have consequences. Charge and trial as adults. It's the best kind of deterrent for other future kids who get bold enough to try something stupid. 

We need to stop coddling prisoners/convicts and showing weakness. All they do time and time again is take advantage and cause more harm and damage in the long run.

author: @MrSecurity.
text: I'm glad they're being charged as adults because in my mind there's no reason anybody that is involved in a serious crime who is over the age of like 10 shouldn't be charged as an adult. I'm just saying when I was 10 years old I knew that murdering people was wrong and it was bad. When you're that young, you know killing is bad. So I'm always over the impression that when one of these young teenagers kills a person or plans to kill a person, they should absolutely always be charged as an adult. I'm not the smartest person in the world by any means, but I still knew when I was young, definitely even younger than 10 that murder was bad. 14 and 15 is definitely old enough to know better. Yeah young people can be stupid but at the end of the day they still know what's right and wrong especially when it comes to killing. I hope they get the maximum penalty.

author: @reneesmith1339
text: I was in Florida prison and I can tell you that there are kids in prison. They call them YO's-young offenders. They were in the same prison as me. They were separate from the adults but we saw them everyday. They make them grow vegetables for the inmates to eat and they are in a boot camp like program so they have no free time, have to work out/march twice a day & have to wear hats, & military like uniforms. It was in Ocala where it's hot AF during the summer. They slept in buildings with no AC (I did too) and they all looked miserable.

author: @ahabsbane
text:
Yukkin it up in the back of a squad. I was young and dumb a long time ago, and I put on a tough face in the cage. Last time I was in the cage, I was a sobbing heaving mess. That was 15+ years ago. It got dropped to a Reckless driving, I blew a 0.08. 

It costed me roughly 10,000 in real cash, and 1-200k in lost potential growth. 

I still have a mark on my record, due to a clerical error (fast track program) a second DUI charge was entered when I paid my fines and fees, and got my license back. Fortunately my boss doesn't give a crap, and I love my job, otherwise it would be worth it to pay and have it wiped out. I did learn my lesson though! I put my keys away before I take that first drink.

Looking back at how much money I spent, yeah, valid crashout! I literally cried when I paid for my last set of tires! 😭
